Free SEO Audit Tool — Frequently Asked Questions

What does this free SEO audit tool check?

The tool runs 50+ automated checks across five categories: meta tags (title, description, headings), page speed and Core Web Vitals, content quality signals, technical SEO (canonical tags, robots.txt, sitemap, SSL), and mobile usability. For Sydney businesses, it also flags local SEO signals like NAP visibility and schema markup.

Is this really free? What's the catch?

Yes, completely free. No credit card, no account required. We built this tool because we want you to understand your SEO situation before committing to any agency — including us. If the audit reveals issues you want help fixing, we offer a no-obligation consultation. That's the only "catch."

How is this different from other free SEO checkers?

Most free tools give you generic scores without context. Our tool provides prioritised action items — ranked by impact — so you know what to fix first. It's also calibrated for Australian websites and Google's local search algorithm, not just generic global SEO metrics.

My score is low. How do I fix the issues?

Start with the Priority Action Items section of your report — those are ranked by impact. Technical issues like missing meta tags and broken redirects can often be fixed quickly. Content and link-building improvements take longer but have the most lasting impact. If you'd prefer expert help, book a free consultation and we'll walk through your results with you.

How often should I audit my website's SEO?

For most Sydney small businesses, a quarterly audit is sufficient to catch regressions. If you're actively doing SEO work — publishing content, building links, making technical changes — run an audit monthly to monitor progress and catch any new issues early.

Does this tool check local SEO for Sydney businesses?

Yes. The audit checks for local SEO signals including schema markup (LocalBusiness), NAP visibility, mobile performance (critical for "near me" searches), and page speed — all of which affect your rankings in Google's local pack and Google Maps results.

Can I audit a competitor's website?

Yes — you can run the tool on any publicly accessible URL. Auditing competitors can reveal gaps they haven't addressed and opportunities for your site to outperform them in Sydney searches.
